name: Royal Pawn Shop
description_short: Chicago-based pawn shop and precious metals buyer featured on Hardcore Pawn: Chicago. Buys, loans, and sells gold, diamonds, silver, platinum, and collectibles.
description_long:
Royal Pawn Shop is a Chicago-based pawn operation run by Randy & Elyse, who are known personalities from the reality TV show Hardcore Pawn: Chicago. The company operates as both a traditional pawn shop offering collateral-based loans and a precious metals/jewelry buyer. According to their website, they accept items of value for either cash purchases or secured loans, with a specific focus on the gold and diamond market in the Chicago area.

The shop specializes in buying and providing loans against precious metals and jewelry, including scrap gold, dental gold, gold watches, class rings, necklaces, wedding rings, broken jewelry, silver (sterling and coin), platinum, diamonds, antique jewelry, luxury watches, and collectibles like gold coins, bullion, and US proof sets. They also buy estate jewelry, designer jewelry, silverware, and antiques. The website emphasizes that they "pay the best prices" and provide cash offers based on current market value, weight, quality, and resale potential of merchandise.

Royal Pawn distinguishes itself through its media presence and local celebrity status in Chicago, leveraging the Hardcore Pawn: Chicago television show to build brand recognition. The website includes educational content about precious metals (gold karats, diamond carats, platinum, silver purity) to help sellers understand what they own. This positions them as more informational than typical pawn shops, though the extent of their competitive pricing advantage versus other Chicago precious metals buyers is not independently verified.

As a pawn shop, Royal Pawn operates under typical pawn industry constraints: loan terms are short-term, redemption rates in the pawn industry are historically low, and interest rates on pawn loans, while legally capped by state regulations, can be substantially higher than traditional lending. The website makes claims about offering "the best prices" but provides no transparent fee structure, loan terms, interest rates, or independent verification of those claims.
address: 305 S Vermont Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90020

company_info.headquarters: 305 S Vermont Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90020

pros: Accepts wide variety of precious metals and jewelry items for immediate cash or loans
Specializes in scrap and broken gold, reducing friction for selling damaged jewelry
Provides educational content on gold karats, diamond carats, and metal purity to inform sellers
Local Chicago presence with named operators (Randy & Elyse) creating accountability and familiarity
Buys dental gold, class rings, and other niche jewelry categories many shops don't accept
Offers both pawn loans (short-term collateral-based) and outright purchase options
TV show media presence may provide additional brand legitimacy and customer base
cons: No transparent fee structure, interest rates, or loan term details published on website—customers must visit or call for pricing
Claims to pay "the best prices" without independent verification or competitive pricing data
Typical pawn loan structure involves short redemption periods and high APRs compared to traditional lending
No clear information on loan-to-value ratios, holding periods, or what happens to unredeemed items
Website disclaimer notes "actors may be used to portray real transactions," raising questions about authenticity of testimonials
best_for: Chicago-area residents needing immediate cash for gold, jewelry, or collectibles without credit checks
Sellers of scrap gold, broken jewelry, or dental gold who want quick liquidation
People seeking short-term collateral loans against precious metals as alternative to payday loans
Estate liquidators or inherited jewelry sellers looking for local Chicago precious metals buyers
diagnosis: Royal Pawn Shop is best for Chicago residents who need fast cash for jewelry or precious metals and prefer immediate payment over the waiting period of mail-in buyers. The main caveat is the lack of transparent pricing and loan terms on their website—customers cannot assess whether rates are competitive without direct contact, and pawn loans typically carry high APRs compared to traditional lending.
services: Gold buying (scrap, broken, watches, necklaces, bracelets, class rings, wedding rings)
Dental gold purchasing (scrap dental gold, old dental gold)
Silver buying (sterling silver, coin silver, silver bars, bullion, candlesticks, silverware)
Gold and platinum bullion and coin purchasing
Pawn loans against jewelry and precious metals (collateral-based)
US proof sets and mint sets purchasing
